candidates.why: Direct top-line revenue label; best match for total revenue/sales.
Also a valid total revenue line, likely duplicate/as-reported version; useful as fallback if labeling changes.

variables.notes: Using revenue lines as a priority list; later code should take the first non-missing value.

candidates.why: Inventory component on the balance sheet, not an income statement cost-of-sales/COGS expense row.

variables.notes: No clear income-statement COGS/cost of sales row is present. 'Raw Materials' is a balance-sheet inventory line and should not be used for COGS.

candidates.why: Operating expense component that appears to be part of overhead, not labeled as COGS, and contributes to operating expenses.
Explicit SG&A label. Even though preview cells are empty, the row exists and may be populated in some years.
Classic SG&A overhead component and clearly separate from depreciation/amortization and finance items.

variables.notes: Selected overhead components only; excluded depreciation/amortization, transfer activities, totals, balancing rows, and finance items. Included explicit SG&A row plus supporting overhead buckets per hierarchy guidance when SG&A row appears mostly empty in preview.
No dedicated research and development expense line is visible in the provided income statement.

candidates.why: Best direct match for interest expense.

candidates.why: Income statement equity-method earnings line, not book equity.
Direct balance-sheet equity line and best available proxy for book equity.
Balancing/subtotal artifact, not appropriate when a direct equity line exists.
Total balance sheet, not equity alone.

variables.notes: No separate parent-only equity line is visible, so 'Total Equity' is used for BE. Accordingly, MIB is left empty to avoid double counting.
